515.desc: Three reviewers, cross-pollinated over three rounds. Enables automated reviews that catch what a single pass misses.

514.desc: Each language becomes a plug-in evaluator. Enables scoring Go/Rust/TS projects; new languages drop in as one class.

510.desc: Records user-approved scope in a lock file build agents check. Enables detecting drift from what was actually approved.

504.desc: One launch-command pattern for all four runners. Enables changing launch behavior in one place instead of four.

501.desc: Stops dumping 2,000 prompt lines into the user message. Enables non-Claude runners to separate instructions from task.

498.desc: Distiller emits structured RFC-2119 specs. Enables specs precise enough to build and review against. One of three copies.

494.desc: Same change as #498/#492 — Akash picks the survivor.

492.desc: Same change as #498/#494 — most precisely scoped description of the three.

493.desc: Parses hypotheses for #refs so the CEO reuses issues instead of duplicating. Code twin of #375.

488.desc: Branches from current main, not stale local. Enables not wasting whole cycles researching outdated code.

457.desc: Clones and checks out the PR branch automatically. Enables pointing the factory straight at a PR.

424.desc: Agents run as background sessions, polled via state files. Enables fleet visibility without tying up a terminal.

423.desc: Formal spec of a future system — work items, contracts, profiles. Enables a shared reference for what the factory should become.

417.desc: Force skips only soft prechecks; hard constraints always block. Enables trusting constraints can't be force-bypassed again.

399.desc: Archive-only research, skipped baseline eval, capped calls. Enables factory cycles at a fraction of the token cost.

391.desc: Maps all 57 modules to roles. Enables fast contributor orientation. (Gate claims only true once #417 lands.)

380.desc: Factory's own backlog label made mature repos look unbuilt. Enables focused runs on established repos without misrouting.

376.desc: Classifies playbook learnings and auto-PRs general ones upstream. Enables every user's improvements flowing back to everyone.

375.desc: Prompt twin of #493 — same goal, lighter mechanism, no code.

374.desc: Instruments dispatch and stream lifecycle. Enables debugging agent problems from logs instead of guesswork.

373.desc: Compiles project state from 9 sources into one brief. Enables resuming or handing off work without re-discovering context.

367.desc: Passes SSH env into tmux, validates up front. Enables agents to git-push over SSH instead of failing silently.

365.desc: factory/fix/slug instead of random hex. Enables telling branches apart at a glance.

288.desc: Wraps every gh call behind a forge interface. Enables running the factory on GitLab-hosted projects.
